provenance.source_corpus: Limiting factors for lodgepole pine (Pinus contorta) and white spruce (Picea glauca) growth differ in some reconstructed sites in the Athabasca oil sands region

source_spans.text: In conclusion, lodgepole pine trees planted on PMM over TS sites were limited by low water availability
In conclusion, lodgepole pine trees planted on PMM over TS sites were limited by low water availability

In conclusion, lodgepole pine trees planted on PMM over TS sites were limited by low water availability, while low soil N availability and high salinity limited the growth of white spruce trees planted on PMM over OB sites.

In conclusion, lodgepole pine trees planted on PMM over TS sites were limited by low water availability, while low soil N availability and high salinity limited the growth of white spruce trees planted on PMM over OB sites.

In conclusion, lodgepole pine trees planted on PMM over TS sites were limited by low water availability, while low soil N availability and high salinity limited the growth of white spruce trees planted on PMM over OB sites.
N availability, salinity and soil compaction combined limited Sw growth on reconstructed sites using the PMM over the OB substrate.
On Pl sites, HG, DBHG and ABG were all positively correlated with VWC
The VWC and dissolved organic carbon (DOC) together explained 80% of the variation in both the current annual increment of height (HI) ( p = 0.007) and aboveground biomass (ABI) ( p = 0.008).
The HG and ABG were negatively correlated with foliar d13C in current-year needles
In Sw sites, HG, DBHG and ABG were all positively correlated with foliar N
In Sw sites, HG, DBHG and ABG were all positively correlated with foliar N, foliar K, and soil inorganic N in PMM
In Sw sites, HG, DBHG and ABG were all positively correlated with foliar N, foliar K, and soil inorganic N in PMM
In Sw sites, HG, DBHG and ABG were all positively correlated with foliar N, foliar K, and soil inorganic N in PMM, and negatively correlated with Db.
The Db and inorganic N concentration together explained 67% of the variation in HI ( p = 0.037), while Db and EC together explained 79% of the variation in ABI ( p = 0.009).
